notes: Pre-birth programs: Foundation, Framing, Durability, Staging+MissionMode
Post-birth programs: Survival, Foundation, Father Athlete
Every exercise includes sets, reps, rest, effort (RPE/RIR), tempo, substitution, and tactile cue
Optional days marked with optional:true can be reward-gated in UI
To use: seed this JSON into your training_programs table in Supabase

pre_birth.name: Transformation · Foundation
pre_birth.focus: Highest-volume block · upper/lower + pump day · build the base while the runway is long

workouts.name: Foundation · Upper Strength A · Week 1
workouts.objective: Heavy horizontal push and pull — the volume anchor of the week.
workouts.coachingNote: First week of the meso: bottom of every range, effort capped. You have more runway right now than you will for years — the program spends it deliberately, not frantically.

exercises.name: Barbell bench press
exercises.cue: Bend the bar apart; touch the sternum and drive the floor away through your feet.

exercises.name: Barbell row
exercises.cue: Hinge to 45 degrees and pull to the lower ribs — torso frozen.

exercises.name: Standing barbell overhead press
exercises.cue: Squeeze glutes hard so the ribs can't flare, then press your head through the window.

exercises.name: Chin-up (weighted if strong)
exercises.effort: Stop 2 reps before failure every set
exercises.cue: Dead-hang start every rep; pull the elbows to the back pockets.

exercises.name: Pallof press
exercises.cue: Press out and refuse the twist for a two-count.

exercises.name: Barbell bench press
exercises.cue: Bend the bar apart; touch the sternum and drive the floor away through your feet.

exercises.name: Standing barbell overhead press
exercises.cue: Squeeze glutes hard so the ribs can't flare, then press your head through the window.

exercises.name: Barbell back squat

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian deadlift
exercises.cue: Push the hips to the wall behind you; the bar drags down the thighs.

exercises.name: Walking lunge
exercises.cue: Short controlled steps; back knee kisses the floor, never crashes.

exercises.name: Standing calf raise
exercises.substitution: Single-leg calf raise off a step
exercises.cue: Pause a full second at the top and the stretch.

exercises.name: Hanging knee raise
exercises.cue: Abs lift the knees; a three-count lowers them.

exercises.name: Incline dumbbell press

exercises.cue: Pull to the eyebrows with the thumbs pointing back.

exercises.name: Hollow hold

pre_birth.name: Transformation · Staging + Mission Mode
pre_birth.focus: 4 days tapering to 3 · RPE ≤7 · arrive at Day One fresh

workouts.name: Staging · Full Body A · Week 1
workouts.objective: Stay strong, stay fresh, stay available — maintenance with zero cost.
workouts.coachingNote: Maintenance only. Thirty-five minutes, RPE 7 ceiling, everything cancelable. A missed session in this phase is the program working, not failing — the freezer meals and the packed bag ARE training.

exercises.name: Goblet squat
exercises.cue: Light, fast, perfect positions.

exercises.name: Dumbbell bench press
exercises.cue: Bend the bar apart; touch the sternum and drive the floor away through your feet.

exercises.name: Chest-supported dumbbell row
exercises.cue: Hinge to 45 degrees and pull to the lower ribs — torso frozen.

exercises.name: Farmer carry
exercises.cue: Hospital-bag weight. Literally.

exercises.cue: In through the nose, out through the brace.

post_birth.name: New Dad Survival
post_birth.focus: Short sessions · low complexity · maintain strength · optional walk/mobility day

workouts.name: Survival A · Week 1
workouts.objective: Squat, push, pull, carry — maintain strength with zero unnecessary fatigue.
workouts.coachingNote: Weeks one and two are for grooving patterns, not chasing effort. If you slept under five hours, drop to Minimum — it counts as a completed day, full stop.

exercises.name: Goblet squat
exercises.cue: Elbows track inside the knees at the bottom.

exercises.name: Dumbbell floor or bench press
exercises.cue: Lower for a full two-count; wrists stacked over elbows.

exercises.name: One-arm dumbbell row
exercises.cue: Pull to the hip pocket, not the armpit.

exercises.name: Farmer carry
exercises.effort: Heavy enough that posture is a fight you win
exercises.substitution: Suitcase carry with one dumbbell, same duration
exercises.cue: This is the car-seat-up-the-stairs rep. Ribs down, knuckles forward.

exercises.name: Dead bug
exercises.cue: Exhale hard and press the lower back into the floor before each rep.

exercises.name: Goblet squat
exercises.cue: Elbows track inside the knees at the bottom.

exercises.name: Dumbbell floor or bench press
exercises.cue: Lower for a full two-count; wrists stacked over elbows.

exercises.name: One-arm dumbbell row
exercises.cue: Pull to the hip pocket, not the armpit.

exercises.name: Farmer carry
exercises.effort: Heavy enough that posture is a fight you win
exercises.substitution: Suitcase carry with one dumbbell, same duration
exercises.cue: This is the car-seat-up-the-stairs rep. Ribs down, knuckles forward.

minimum.format: 3 rounds with one dumbbell, rest as needed

workouts.name: Survival B · Week 1
workouts.objective: Hinge, press, pull, carry — the other half of the weekly movement diet.
workouts.coachingNote: Weeks one and two are for grooving patterns, not chasing effort. If you slept under five hours, drop to Minimum — it counts as a completed day, full stop.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian deadlift
exercises.substitution: Hip hinge with a loaded backpack
exercises.cue: Push the hips back until the hamstrings load; the dumbbells slide down the thighs.

exercises.name: Half-kneeling one-arm press
exercises.cue: Squeeze the down-knee glute so the ribs can't flare.

exercises.name: Gorilla row
exercises.cue: Hinge and hold; row without the torso twisting.

exercises.name: Suitcase carry
exercises.effort: Heavy on one side, spine dead level
exercises.cue: One-arm stroller push, groceries in the other hand — resist the lean.

exercises.name: Side plank
exercises.cue: Stack shoulder over elbow and push the floor away.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian deadlift
exercises.substitution: Hip hinge with a loaded backpack
exercises.cue: Push the hips back until the hamstrings load; the dumbbells slide down the thighs.

exercises.name: Half-kneeling one-arm press
exercises.cue: Squeeze the down-knee glute so the ribs can't flare.

exercises.name: Suitcase carry
exercises.effort: Heavy on one side, spine dead level
exercises.cue: One-arm stroller push, groceries in the other hand — resist the lean.

exercises.name: Side plank
exercises.cue: Stack shoulder over elbow and push the floor away.

minimum.format: 3 rounds with one dumbbell, rest as needed

workouts.name: Survival · Optional Recovery Day · Week 1
workouts.objective: Sanctioned third day: walk, mobility, grip. Zero fatigue, real value.
workouts.coachingNote: Optional means optional. Take it when you need to move but the body can't take another strength session — after a bad night, or on a day off from A/B. It never breaks a streak; it only builds one.

exercises.name: Easy walk — flat or slight incline
exercises.substitution: Push the stroller for the same duration
exercises.cue: Head up, phone in the pocket. This is training AND transport.

exercises.name: Cat-cow
exercises.cue: Move one vertebra at a time; the breath sets the tempo.

exercises.name: Deep squat hold
exercises.cue: Elbows push knees out; sit at the bottom like you're waiting on the baby.

exercises.name: 90/90 hip switches
exercises.cue: Switch legs without the hands helping; hips do the work.

exercises.name: Grip: dumbbell hold or towel hang
exercises.effort: Heavy enough to be a challenge, not a rescue mission
exercises.substitution: Bag hold at your side, 30 sec
exercises.cue: Crush hard, breathe calm — grip is a fatherhood skill.

workouts.name: Foundation A · Week 7
workouts.objective: Squat-focus full body with a conditioning finisher — progressive workload, one variable at a time.
workouts.coachingNote: Structured training is back: three days, A/B/C, one rep or one load jump per week. The program does the thinking so your fried brain doesn't have to.

exercises.name: Goblet squat
exercises.cue: Break at the hips and knees together; heels stay welded down.

exercises.name: Dumbbell bench press
exercises.cue: Drag the dumbbells apart like you're tearing paper — lats on.

exercises.name: One-arm dumbbell row
exercises.cue: Let the shoulder blade reach at the bottom, then drive it to the spine.

exercises.name: Farmer carry
exercises.effort: Heavier than Survival — posture is the rep
exercises.cue: Walk like both hands are full and the daycare door still has to open.

exercises.name: Dead bug
exercises.cue: Opposite arm and leg lower only as far as the lower back stays pinned.

exercises.name: Conditioning finisher — dumbbell + rope or jog
exercises.effort: Hard but repeatable — RPE 8 breathing, crisp movement
exercises.cue: 40 sec work / 20 sec rest: swings or squat-to-press, alternate with rope or run in place.

workouts.coachingNote: Lighter week: one set less on the main work, same weights as week one, everything smooth. This is where the last three weeks turn into strength. Then the cycle repeats, heavier.

exercises.name: Front-loaded squat (dumbbells or barbell)
exercises.effort: RPE 6 · lighter week — same load as week 1, crisp reps
exercises.cue: Elbows up, brace like you're about to be poked in the stomach, then sit between the heels.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian deadlift
exercises.effort: RPE 6 · move well, leave plenty in the tank
exercises.cue: Load the hamstrings before the dumbbells ever move — hinge is a pre-load, not a drop.

exercises.name: Walking lunge
exercises.effort: RPE 6 · move well, leave plenty in the tank
exercises.cue: Short, controlled steps; the back knee kisses the floor, never crashes.

exercises.name: Farmer carry
exercises.effort: Heavy — this is the airport carry, trained
exercises.cue: Car seat in one hand, duffel in the other, gate closing. Walk tall anyway.

exercises.name: Hanging knee raise or dead bug
exercises.cue: Pull the knees up with the abs, not momentum; lower on a three-count.

exercises.name: Front-loaded squat (dumbbells or barbell)
exercises.effort: RPE 6 · lighter week — same load as week 1, crisp reps
exercises.cue: Elbows up, brace like you're about to be poked in the stomach, then sit between the heels.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian deadlift
exercises.effort: RPE 6 · move well, leave plenty in the tank
exercises.cue: Load the hamstrings before the dumbbells ever move — hinge is a pre-load, not a drop.

exercises.name: Farmer carry
exercises.effort: Heavy — this is the airport carry, trained
exercises.cue: Car seat in one hand, duffel in the other, gate closing. Walk tall anyway.

minimum.format: AMRAP 8 minutes with one dumbbell, steady pace

workouts.name: Father Athlete · Upper · Week 4
workouts.objective: Upper-body strength anchor — heavy push and pull in balance.
workouts.coachingNote: Lighter week: one set less on the main work, same weights as week one, everything smooth. This is where the last three weeks turn into strength. Then the cycle repeats, heavier.

exercises.name: Dumbbell bench press (or barbell)
exercises.effort: RPE 6 · lighter week — same load as week 1, crisp reps
exercises.cue: Bend the bar / pull the dumbbells apart; touch and drive without bouncing.

exercises.name: Chest-supported dumbbell row
exercises.effort: RPE 6 · move well, leave plenty in the tank
exercises.cue: Pause a full second at the top — the squeeze is the rep.

exercises.name: Half-kneeling one-arm press
exercises.effort: RPE 6 · move well, leave plenty in the tank
exercises.cue: Down-knee glute locked; press slightly back so the biceps finishes by the ear.

exercises.name: Chin-up or band pulldown
exercises.effort: Stop 2 reps before failure every set
exercises.cue: Start every rep from a dead hang; chest to the bar, not chin to the sky.

exercises.name: Pallof press
exercises.cue: Ribs stacked over hips; the band moves, you don't.

workouts.name: Father Athlete · Carry + Engine · Week 4
workouts.objective: The dad-strength day: heavy carries in every style plus the week's conditioning block.
workouts.coachingNote: Lighter week: one set less on the main work, same weights as week one, everything smooth. This is where the last three weeks turn into strength. Then the cycle repeats, heavier.

exercises.name: Dumbbell deadlift
exercises.effort: RPE 6 · lighter week — same load as week 1, crisp reps
exercises.cue: Wedge the hips down and back, then stand up through the whole foot.

exercises.name: Loaded carry medley — farmer → suitcase → overhead
exercises.effort: Heavy but position-perfect; downgrade the weight, never the posture
exercises.cue: Farmer: knuckles forward. Suitcase: belt level. Overhead: biceps by the ear, ribs down.

exercises.name: Conditioning block — bike, hill walk, or dumbbell complex
exercises.effort: RPE 8 breathing — hard but repeatable, nasal-breathing-broken
exercises.substitution: Stair climbs or brisk stroller push
exercises.cue: Intervals: 60 sec work / 60 sec easy. Same output every round is the goal.

exercises.name: Side plank
exercises.cue: Push the floor away and make a straight line from ankle to armpit.

exercises.effort: RPE 6 · move well, leave plenty in the tank
exercises.cue: Slower on the way down than up — three seconds of brakes, one of gas.

exercises.name: Incline dumbbell press
exercises.effort: RPE 6 · move well, leave plenty in the tank

exercises.name: Lateral raise + rear-delt fly superset

exercises.name: Farmer carry finisher
exercises.cue: End the week the way it started: standing under load, breathing calm.

workouts.name: Father Athlete · 5-Day · Arms + Delts + Carries · Week 4
workouts.objective: Fifth-day accelerant for the 5-day variant: arms, delts, and skill-of-strength carries.
workouts.coachingNote: Only appears if you selected the 5-day Father Athlete variant. If you miss any of days 1–4 this week, skip Day 5 — never the strength work. This is the day that raises weekly volume toward 14–16 sets/muscle.
